ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] Make your own "My Fairy Tales" and/ or "Tall Tales" book together. Let student dictate what they want you to write as narration of the scene or as captions to their brush drawings. Bind the illustrations and narrations into a story book.
[ ] Act out favorite stories for family and friends.
[ ] Look for local theater or dance companies performing these stories.

[ ] Look for different versions of a classic fairy tale like Cinderella or Goldilocks at your library. Compare the versions and discuss what you like best about each.
[ ] Ask family and friends about their favorite fairy tale or tall tale. Are any mentioned more than once? Are there any you would like to read?
[ ] Create a new ending (or an entirely different plot) for a fairy tale or tall tale you have read.

[ ] Use blocks, Legos, cardboard, or other building materials to create a scene from a fairy tale or tall tale you have read.
[ ] Create a wanted poster for a villain from one of the tales. What was their crime? How can they be identified? What reward will be given for their capture? Be sure to include a picture.
[ ] Retell a story you have read from a different point of view. For example, you could tell a story about Paul Bunyan from the point of view of Babe or his axe.
